title: T-016 — LP create + per-warehouse numbering service (WH §6.3)
prompt:
# T-016 — LP create + per-warehouse numbering service (WH §6.3)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §6.3
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement `createLicensePlate` service that allocates `lp_number = settings.lp_number_prefix + zero-padded sequence` per (org_id, warehouse_id) via a Postgres sequence, with optional manual override gated by `allow_manual_lp_number`.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/lp-create.ts`: function `createLicensePlate(input, ctx)` returning created LP. Allocates `lp_number` via Postgres sequence `lp_number_seq_{org}_{warehouse}` (lazily created) and pads to `lp_number_sequence_length` (default 8).
2. Manual override accepted only when `settings.allow_manual_lp_number=true`; uniqueness re-validated via `UNIQUE(org_id, warehouse_id, lp_number)`.
3. Emits no outbox event directly — caller (GRN/output) emits via T-047 wiring.
4. Returns typed Drizzle insert result.

## Files
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/lp-create.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/lp-create.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a fresh warehouse, when `createLicensePlate` is called 3 times, then `lp_number` values are 'LP00000001', 'LP00000002', 'LP00000003' (per settings).
2. Given two warehouses in the same tenant, when each gets its first LP, then both are 'LP00000001' (per-warehouse sequence, not per-tenant).
3. Given `allow_manual_lp_number=false`, when a manual `lp_number='CUSTOM-1'` is supplied, then the service rejects with a clear error.
4. Given `allow_manual_lp_number=true` and a duplicate manual number, when supplied, then the DB unique constraint surfaces a 409-equivalent service error.

## Test strategy
- RED: `apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/lp-create.test.ts` asserting per-warehouse sequence isolation + manual override gating.
- Run `pnpm --filter web test lp-create`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not use a single global sequence — per-warehouse is required.
- Do not bypass `allow_manual_lp_number` gate.
- Do not call outbox emit here — T-047 owns wiring.

## Dependencies
- T-002
- T-009
- T-012

Allocates `lp_number` via Postgres sequence `lp_number_seq_{org}_{warehouse}` (lazily created) and pads to `lp_number_sequence_length` (default 8). Manual override accepted only when `settings.allow_manual_lp_number=true`; uniqueness re-validated via `UNIQUE(org_id, warehouse_id, lp_number)`. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-tenant-context-remediation.md`).
acceptance_criteria: Given a fresh warehouse, when `createLicensePlate` is called 3 times, then `lp_number` values are 'LP00000001', 'LP00000002', 'LP00000003' (per settings).
Given two warehouses in the same tenant, when each gets its first LP, then both are 'LP00000001' (per-warehouse sequence, not per-tenant).
Given `allow_manual_lp_number=false`, when a manual `lp_number='CUSTOM-1'` is supplied, then the service rejects with a clear error.
Given `allow_manual_lp_number=true` and a duplicate manual number, when supplied, then the DB unique constraint surfaces a 409-equivalent service error.
test_strategy: RED: `apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/lp-create.test.ts` asserting per-warehouse sequence isolation + manual override gating.
risk_red_lines: Do not read current_setting('app.tenant_id') or current_setting('app.current_org_id') directly — use the foundation app.current_org_id() function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
Do not use tenant_id as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is org_id. See _meta/audits/2026-05-14-tenant-context-remediation.md.
Do not use a single global sequence — per-warehouse is required.
Do not call outbox emit here — T-047 owns wiring.
